claims.evidence_text: Application of sulfur fertilizers at 45 kg S ha-1 proved to be efficient in maximizing many parameters like plant height, tiller count, chlorophyll, LAI, dry matter accumulation and yield
claims.claim_summary: Sulfur applied at 45 kg S/ha maximized wheat growth parameters and yield (best of 0/15/30/45 levels tested).

claims.evidence_text: Sulfur requirements for one tonne of seeds range from 1-6 kg for cereals, 5-13 kg for legumes and 5-20 kg for oilseed crops.
claims.claim_summary: Cereals (incl. wheat) require 1-6 kg sulfur per tonne of seed produced.

claims.evidence_text: Recommended dose of nitrogen, phosphorus and potassium (N:P:K-125:62.5:0) kg ha-1 in the form of urea, di-ammonium phosphate was applied. Half dose of nitrogen... applied as basal dose and the other half... at the time of 1st irrigation.
claims.claim_summary: Wheat received 125 kg N/ha (urea/DAP), split half basal and half at first irrigation.

claims.evidence_text: Recommended dose of nitrogen, phosphorus and potassium (N:P:K-125:62.5:0) kg ha-1... full dose of phosphorus were applied as basal dose
claims.claim_summary: Wheat received 62.5 kg P/ha applied as basal dose.

claims.evidence_text: Half dose of nitrogen, full dose of phosphorus were applied as basal dose and the other half of nitrogen was applied at the time of 1st irrigation.
claims.claim_summary: Nitrogen on wheat split: half basal at sowing, half at first irrigation.

claims.evidence_text: 45 kg S ha-1 reported to show the maximum test weight (45.06 g) and grains per ear (44.8) at harvest. The magnitude of increase in test weight... were 12.28 and 15.83 % in L4 (45 kg S ha-1) when compared to L1 (0 kg S ha-1)
claims.claim_summary: Wheat 1000-grain test weight reached 45.06 g (g per 1000 kernels) at 45 kg S/ha, a 12.3% increase over no sulfur.

claims.evidence_text: 45 kg S ha-1 reported to show the maximum test weight (45.06 g) and grains per ear (44.8) at harvest
claims.claim_summary: Wheat grains per ear reached 44.8 at 45 kg S/ha (15.8% increase over zero-sulfur control).

claims.evidence_text: Spacing of 22.5 cm row to row was followed.
claims.claim_summary: Wheat sown at 22.5 cm row spacing.

claims.evidence_text: Significantly the highest plant height (55.2, 83.7 cm) was obtained in 45 kg S ha-1 at 60 and 90 DAS
claims.claim_summary: Wheat plant height reached 83.7 cm at 90 DAS with 45 kg S/ha (vs 76.8 cm at zero sulfur).

claims.evidence_text: Significant results in LAI were seen at 60 DAS with Bentonite showing the highest LAI (2.67) at 60 DAS
claims.claim_summary: Wheat leaf area index reached ~2.67 at 60 DAS under sulfur (Bentonite source).
